links.label: The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God
looked down from heaven upon the children of men
there is none that doeth good, no, not one
who eat up my people as they eat bread
There were they in great fear
Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion

name: The Fool Hath Said
notes: Psalm 14 and Psalm 53 are near-duplicate laments over universal corruption, aligning verse for verse (both superscriptions counted as verse 1). The wording diverges most sharply at the fifth verse: Psalm 14:5-6 speaks of the fear of the righteous and the shaming of the poor's counsel, while Psalm 53:5 instead has God scattering the bones of the besieger. Where divergence is too great for an exact shared phrase, the label is omitted.
